description_long: This rock is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a rugged, uneven surface and a mix of gray, white, and dark mineral streaks. The texture appears coarse, with jagged edges and a somewhat flattened profile on one side, suggesting it could have been naturally weathered or broken from a larger formation. The coloration is mottled, with dominant shades of light gray and white interspersed with darker, almost black patches, giving it a marbled appearance. The rock's surface is mostly matte and rough, with no visible polish or gloss, and it lacks any artificial markings or modifications. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area. There are no visible cavities or openings, and the rock appears solid and dense throughout.
description: A rugged, irregularly shaped gray and white rock with a coarse, marbled surface and jagged edges. It features a mix of light and dark mineral streaks and a rough, matte finish.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest, flattest side of the rock, highlighting its marbled gray and white coloration with prominent dark streaks.
description_view_1: The rock appears narrow and pointed from this angle, emphasizing its jagged, uneven edge and vertical profile.
description_view_2: This side reveals a smoother, more uniform gray surface with subtle white veining and less pronounced dark patches.
description_view_3: Seen from this view, the rock is tall and thin, with a rough, textured surface and a mix of gray and white tones.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
